---
title: "tracker_explore"
author: "Holden Jones"
date: "2026-04-21"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(baseballr)
library(tidyverse)
```

define game_ID searching function
```{r}
# Function to fetch GameID (game_pk)
get_my_mlb_id <- function(game_date, home_team_name) {
  
  # 1. Fetch all games for that date
  message(paste("Searching for:", home_team_name, "on", game_date))
  day_games <- get_game_pks_mlb(date = game_date)
  
  # 2. Filter for the home team
  # Note: Use grepl for partial matches (e.g., "Red Sox" instead of "Boston Red Sox")
  specific_game <- day_games %>%
    filter(grepl(home_team_name, teams.home.team.name, ignore.case = TRUE))
  
  # 3. Error handling: check if we found exactly one game
  if (nrow(specific_game) == 0) {
    return(NA) # Game not found
  } else if (nrow(specific_game) > 1) {
    # Handles doubleheaders - default to the first game
    return(specific_game$game_pk[1])
  } else {
    return(specific_game$game_pk)
  }
}
```

```{r}
# Load your manual file
my_data <- read.csv("data/game_log.csv")

# Apply the function to every row
# This creates a new column 'game_ID' by looking at 'date' and 'home_team'
my_data$game_ID <- mapply(get_my_mlb_id, my_data$date, my_data$home_team)

# View the result
head(my_data)

# Save it back so you don't have to ping the API again
write.csv(my_data, "my_watched_games_updated.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```
